page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9a.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps. This instance does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Additionally, there are no integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated core allocation ensures predictable CPU performance without resource contention. The balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a stable hardware profile for workloads that do not require specialized accelerators or high-speed local storage. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance as a cost-efficient option for standard compute and memory tasks where external storage is preferred.

The hardware configuration is suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, build systems, and medium-sized database deployments that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9a.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps. Tradeoffs include the absence of local storage and GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network volumes. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance and balanced memory allocation, such as enterprise application hosting, containerized deployments, and web serving.
